dimensions.name: num_interactions
dimensions.comment: Number of user interactions in the session

config.comment: Session-level human friction metrics - only sessions with enough tool decisions to be meaningful

dimensions.name: primary_model
dimensions.comment: Primary model used in the session

config.source:
SELECT
  t.session_id,
  t.primary_model,
  t.tool_name,
  t.num_tool_decisions,
  t.num_tool_rejects,
  t.num_tool_accepts,
  t.reject_rate,
  s.session_start,
  s.signal_strength
FROM tanner_fevm_catalog.claude_gold.session_human_signals_by_tool t JOIN tanner_fevm_catalog.claude_gold.session_human_signals s
  ON t.session_id = s.session_id
config.comment: Per-tool human signal breakdown - which tools get rejected most

measures.name: sessions_with_signal
measures.expr: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN signal_strength = true THEN session_id END)

measures.name: signal_coverage_rate
measures.expr:
COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN signal_strength = true THEN session_id END) * 1.0
/ COUNT(DISTINCT session_id)

config.comment: All sessions including weak-signal ones for overall coverage KPIs

dimensions.name: signal_strength
dimensions.comment: Whether session has enough tool decisions to be meaningful

lines: Behavioral friction metrics derived from user interactions — tool rejections, aborts, and corrections. Cheap to compute and reflects real user experience. Use to detect regressions after model upgrades.

frame.title: Tool Rejection Rate by Tool & Model

lines: Scoring across 5 dimensions (Efficiency 20%, Productivity 25%, Quality 20%, Autonomy 15%, Engagement 20%) plus LLM-as-judge evaluation
